_comment: SOURCE MATERIAL for the M5 acceptance demo -- not a demo itself. Renders 12 s of percussive, bright, bit-crushed stabs, deliberately UNLIKE the sustained chord phrase the synth part plays in the demo. The point: granulating a synth drone with a synth playing over it makes the two roles indistinguishable. Transient-rich, chromatic, digitally gritty material granulates into glass and dust instead of into a second pad, so 'the synth makes the music, the sampler makes the room' becomes audible. Render this to host/render/scenarios/assets/in_percussive.wav.

init._comment: part B silent -- only A's material goes into the asset
hot and steady: percussive material has a low RMS by nature, so run LEVEL at the top with no modulation -- the asset should use its headroom, the deck sets the level later
dense 16-step stabs, some variation so it is not a loop
percussive voice: instant attack, shortest decay, bright, wide detune
bright register, chromatic -- deliberately less tonal than the demo phrase
bit reduction: digital grit, so the grains read as glass rather than as drone
bone dry -- the room must come from the deck, not be baked into its source
